text:
I'm so happy to hear of those things. And you notice, after I'm gone from here a long time… And you ministers in your churches, you'll hear people appearing, and saying, "You know, that's just left me." There's many of them that I can't call. I see it hanging all over them. And in between here, anyone who stands here on the platform, it's a dark place in between here.
And sometimes, I see the move, but I can't see the person, see, just right. And especially when you're under anointing, you just don't see it. And then, if it don't act quickly, well, it'll move somewhere else, you see. And then, when I'm standing here at the platform, I feel it while it's here, and then it'll go out. I'll see it, hang---watch it, and it'll hang over someone, see, and then a vision will open up.
And I know there's been many, many people that's never even been here, or anywhere in this line, has been healed here. And I don't even call them from the platform here. I just start… Usually, right down to the last of the service (it seems like sometimes) just a great collapse of faith just moves everywhere, you see. And there's many of them that accepts it, see.
And as far as your healing, why, I know that Christ healed everyone when He died for them, you see. So the only thing, it's just your faith to believe it.
text_no:
Jeg er så glad for å høre om disse tingene. Og dere merker, etter at jeg har vært borte herfra en lang tid... Og dere predikanter i deres menigheter, dere vil høre folk si: "Du vet, det har bare forlatt meg." Det er mange av dem jeg ikke kan kalle. Jeg ser det henge over dem. Og her imellom, alle som står her på plattformen, det er et mørkt område imellom.
Noen ganger ser jeg bevegelsen, men jeg kan ikke se personen helt klart, spesielt når du er under salving. Du ser det ikke tydelig. Og hvis det ikke handler raskt, vil det bevege seg et annet sted. Når jeg står her på plattformen, føler jeg det mens det er her, og så går det ut. Jeg ser det henge over noen, og så åpner en visjon seg.
Jeg vet at det har vært mange, mange mennesker som aldri har vært her, eller i denne bønnekøen, men som har blitt helbredet her. Jeg kaller dem ikke engang fra plattformen. Vanligvis, mot slutten av møtet, ser det ut til å være en stor utgytelse av tro overalt. Og mange av dem aksepterer det.
Når det gjelder din helbredelse, vet jeg at Kristus helbredet alle da Han døde for dem. Så det eneste som trengs, er deres tro for å tro det.

text:
A while ago, there was a minister drove in close to where I'm staying. And he introduced himself. And he prays for the sick, also. And he has a case of a sick girl. She's awful bad.
I said, "Slip on up, brother, up to the meeting." I said, "They may give out cards, today." We didn't give them out, however, this afternoon.
And he said, "Brother Branham, we don't want a prayer card. We just want to watch. It's the inspiration of the Lord what brings us up to the healing."
That man's right. He's got the idea. That's just what it takes, you see. It's the inspiration of it, brings you up to that place where you see what God's doing. His presence is there.
Now, do you know that God goes in certain places, and then He'll be somewhere else, and He'll be somewhere else? Did you know that? Didn't hear very many "Amens," but that's the truth, see. And the presence of the Lord was there to heal the people. Is that right? And the Lord met Moses in the way." You remember? Met with him in the way, when he went out.
text_no:
For en tid tilbake kom en forkynner kjørende nær der jeg bor og introduserte seg selv. Han ber også for syke og har en sak med en alvorlig syk jente.
Jeg sa: "Kom med, Bror, opp til møtet." Jeg sa: "De kan dele ut kort i dag." Vi delte imidlertid ikke ut noen kort denne ettermiddagen.
Han sa: "Bror Branham, vi ønsker ikke et bønnekort. Vi vil bare observere. Det er Herrens inspirasjon som leder oss til helbredelsen."
Den mannen har forstått det riktig. Det er nettopp det som kreves, ser du. Det er inspirasjonen som bringer deg til det stedet hvor du ser hva Gud gjør. Hans nærvær er der.
Visste du at Gud besøker bestemte steder, deretter et annet sted, og så et annet sted? Visste du det? Jeg hørte ikke mange "Amen", men det er sannheten. Herrens nærvær var der for å helbrede folket. Er det ikke riktig? Og Herren møtte Moses på veien. Husker du? Han møtte ham på veien da han dro ut.

text:
A lady in my church … Brother Beeler. Mrs. Webber---she was dying with TB. She was in a sanitarium at the last stages. They sent her home to die. Said there was nothing could be done for her---Mrs. Grace Webber in Jeffersonville. She lived just beyond the tabernacle. She's got five or six little children.
So, the Angel of the Lord come to me that night and said, "Go tell Mrs. Webber … tell Mr. Webber, rather, to get things ready, for he's going to be left with those children on his hands, for his wife's going."
Well, I went and told Mr. Webber. I told his little girl, little Jean Rose, which is a nurse, now. She was a little bitty fellow then; she's a young lady now. It's been seven years ago, or more. I said, "Now, Jeanie, your mother's going to die. She can't live but a little while longer."
text_no:
En dame i min menighet … Bror Beeler. Fru Webber—hun holdt på å dø av tuberkulose. Hun var i en sanatorium i siste stadier. De sendte henne hjem for å dø, og sa at ingenting kunne gjøres for henne. Fru Grace Webber i Jeffersonville bodde rett forbi tabernaklet. Hun hadde fem eller seks små barn.
Den natten kom Herrens Engel til meg og sa: "Gå og fortell Fru Webber ... fortell heller Mr. Webber å gjøre klar tingene, for han kommer til å bli alene med barna, da hans kone kommer til å gå bort."
Jeg gikk og fortalte Mr. Webber. Jeg fortalte dette også til deres lille datter, Jean Rose, som nå er en sykepleier. Hun var en liten jente den gangen; nå er hun en ung dame. Dette var for syv år siden, eller mer. Jeg sa: "Nå, Jeanie, moren din kommer til å dø. Hun kan bare leve en liten stund til."

text:
And two days after that, or three days, there was some ladies from the government depot, there where she worked, come in. And Grace (Mrs. Webber) said, "If I could only have Brother Bill to pray for me once more." She said, "I seen when my cousin Opal was healed of that cancer. The doctor just give her till morning to live." And her daughter's a nurse, also.
And the girls from out the government where she worked, said, "There ain't nothing to that guy." Said, "He isn't nothing but a hypocrite." Said, "That's all that religion is, is just a bunch of fake fanaticism."
And so, Mrs. Webber said, "Look. I'm dying and I know that, but I just won't stand still for that." She said, "I know better than that." She said, "I've been living right here around this city, around that man all the time. I seen it from a child, growed up." She said, "I know that you can call it fake if you want to," she said, "but I've seen it just so much, and seen God heal the people." She said, "And I know the man's life, and I know it's the truth."
text_no:
To eller tre dager etter det kom noen damer fra statens kontor, der hun jobbet. Grace (Mrs. Webber) sa: "Om jeg bare kunne få Bror Bill til å be for meg en gang til." Hun sa: "Jeg så da min kusine Opal ble helbredet fra kreften. Legen ga henne ikke annet enn natten å leve." Hennes datter er også sykepleier.
Jentene fra statens kontor, der hun jobbet, sa: "Det er ingenting med den fyren. Han er bare en hykler. Hele denne religionen er bare en haug med falsk fanatisme."
Mrs. Webber svarte: "Se her. Jeg dør, og det vet jeg, men jeg kommer ikke til å godta det der. Jeg vet bedre enn det." Hun fortsatte: "Jeg har bodd her i byen og rundt den mannen hele tiden. Jeg har sett ham fra jeg var barn og vokste opp. Dere kan kalle det falskt om dere vil," sa hun, "men jeg har sett nok til å se Gud helbrede folk. Jeg kjenner mannens liv, og jeg vet det er sannheten."

text:
And it happened to be, the Angel of the Lord heard that. And that very night, sitting on the side of my chair in the room, after I got up and went and got a drink of water about three o'clock in the morning. And sitting on a chair, I seen Him come walking through the door. He said, "Tomorrow (Sunday), that they're going to pick Mrs. Webber up, and bring her down. She'll be sitting on the right-hand side, far back in the tabernacle." Said, "I heard her. And tell her I heard her---what she said. And tell her, 'Thus saith the Lord, she'll live and not die.'"
There you are. You go down and see her, if you'd like to. It's been about seven years ago.
All right, see. His presence was there. When she was taking up for what was right, see, His presence was there, and He found her. So He come and told me what she had said---what she said to these women---and He had respect to her, because she had respect unto what was representing God.
So, God'll do the same thing for you. Not to respect to me, but to respect to Him---to Christ---the One who died for your healing. If you'll just give respects to Him, and His Word, God'll do the work for you. Amen. That is right.
text_no:
Og det skjedde at Herrens Engel hørte det. Samme natt, rundt klokken tre om morgenen, satt jeg på en stol i rommet etter å ha stått opp og hentet meg et glass vann. Mens jeg satt der, så jeg Ham komme gående gjennom døren. Han sa: "I morgen (søndag) vil de hente fru Webber og bringe henne hit. Hun vil sitte på høyre side, langt bak i tabernaklet." Han sa: "Jeg hørte henne. Fortell henne at Jeg hørte hva hun sa. Og fortell henne: 'Så sier Herren, hun skal leve og ikke dø.'"
Der har du det. Du kan dra ned og besøke henne hvis du vil. Det er omtrent syv år siden dette skjedde.
Her ser du, Hans nærvær var til stede. Da hun sto for det som var rett, var Hans nærvær der, og Han fant henne. Så Han kom og fortalte meg hva hun hadde sagt til disse kvinnene, og Han viste henne respekt fordi hun hadde respekt for det som representerte Gud.
Gud vil gjøre det samme for deg. Ikke på grunn av respekt for meg, men på grunn av respekt for Ham — Kristus — Han som døde for din helbredelse. Hvis du bare viser Ham og Hans Ord respekt, vil Gud utføre verket for deg. Amen. Det er riktig.

text:
Now, tonight, we want to talk a little bit from the Word. And this afternoon, I think I kind of preached… [Break in tape.] … for he's going to go in a heart attack. I seen my father come down and mark out his place, and told me where it was, and where he was going. I had a dream… No, standing, looking at these things, I'm looking at these other things that happened.
And the boy was perfectly strong, and healthy, and everything. A few nights ago, fell across the table in a heart attack, and was laying at… Well, he's just barely living now, see. And there's no need of praying. His time is set, and he knows it, and he's going to go, see. He's my brother.
Now, if I was a healer, I'd go heal him, wouldn't I? That's my brother. But, I'm not the healer. And if there's any message of courage I could tell him, I'd like to go tell him that the only thing that I can say is he's going to die, for that's what God told me, see. And he will. That's right.
So now, see, if I was a healer, God knows I'd sure go heal my own brother. Yes, sir. But, I can't do it. That's all. And God has even… The only thing I know, he might, as Hezekiah of old, turn his face to the wall, and weep and tell God what he would do. And God might spare him that way. But, that I don't know; that's between him and God. The only thing I can do is deliver the message, just like He told me.
text_no:
I kveld ønsker vi å snakke litt om Ordet. I ettermiddag tror jeg at jeg på en måte forkynte... [Tomt.område.på.lydbånd] ... for han kommer til å få et hjerteinfarkt. Jeg så min far komme ned og merke ut hans plass, og fortelle meg hvor det var og hvor han skulle. Jeg hadde en drøm... Nei, jeg sto og så på disse tingene, og tenkte på det som skjedde.
Gutten var helt sterk og sunn, men for noen netter siden falt han over et bord med et hjerteinfarkt og lå der... Vel, han lever knapt nå, ser du. Det er ingen vits i å be. Tiden hans er fastsatt, og han vet det. Han skal gå, ser du. Han er min bror.
Hvis jeg var en helbreder, ville jeg gå og helbrede ham, ikke sant? Det er min bror. Men jeg er ikke helbrederen. Hvis det var noen oppmuntrende melding jeg kunne gi ham, ville jeg gjerne gjort det, men det eneste jeg kan si, er at han skal dø, for det er det Gud har fortalt meg. Og det vil han. Det er riktig.
Så nå, ser du, hvis jeg var en helbreder, vet Gud at jeg ville helbredet min egen bror. Ja, herre. Men jeg kan ikke gjøre det. Det er alt. Gud har til og med... Det eneste jeg vet, er at han kanskje, som Esekias i gamle dager, vender ansiktet mot veggen, gråter og forteller Gud hva han ville gjort. Gud kan spare ham på den måten. Men det vet jeg ikke; det er mellom ham og Gud. Det eneste jeg kan gjøre, er å levere meldingen, akkurat som Gud fortalte meg.

text:
Now, I want to read some out of II Kings. Who likes to hear the old-fashioned, sassafras, cornbread and beans type of preaching. Do you like it? Just the old-fashioned type. Well, now, I'm not very much of a preacher, but I just … I like it the old-fashioned way. That's the only way I know it. It saved me. And I tell you, it'll be good for you. It'll save you. It won't polish you very much. It won't white-wash you, but it'll wash you white. Yes, sir.
I believe in one of those old-fashioned, backwoods, sky-blue, sin-killing religion. That's right. I believe it makes us all one in Christ Jesus. It makes a silk dress, and a calico put their arms around one another, and call one another, "sister." It'll make a tuxedo suit, and overalls hug one anther, and call "brother." That's right. It'll make us all the same.
So, it saved John Wesley, and Dwight Moody, and Sankey, and Finney, and Knox, and Paul, and Peter, and James, and John, and all… Well, it's good enough for me. That's all.
text_no:
La oss lese fra Andre Kongebok. Hvem liker å høre gammeldags, enkel forkynnelse? Liker du det? Bare den gammeldagse typen. Vel, jeg er kanskje ikke den beste forkynneren, men jeg liker det på den gammeldagse måten. Det er den eneste måten jeg kjenner. Den frelste meg, og jeg kan fortelle deg at den vil være bra for deg også. Den vil ikke pynte på deg, men den vil vaske deg ren. Ja, det kan du stole på.
Jeg tror på de gamle, enkle, hjerterensende budskapene. Det stemmer. Jeg tror det gjør oss alle ett i Kristus Jesus. Det får en silkekjole og en rutete kjole til å legge armene rundt hverandre og kalle hverandre "søster." Det får en smoking og en kjeledress til å omfavne hverandre og kalle hverandre "bror." Det stemmer. Det gjør oss alle like.
Så, denne forkynnelsen frelste John Wesley, Dwight Moody, Sankey, Finney, Knox, Paulus, Peter, Jakob og Johannes, og alle de andre... Vel, det er godt nok for meg. Det er alt.

text:
Now, over in II Kings, the third chapter, beginning with the tenth verse, to read just a few portions of Scripture, here, or verses, rather. And we'll get right to the healing line as quick as possible … or, not the healing line. The healing line is at Calvary. We'll get to praying for the sick.
This was a great day in the days of Israel. Just in a time of decision---about like we stand today. A broken up, un… discontented bunch of people. Listen:
And the King of Israel said, Alas! … the LORD has called these … kings together, to deliver them into the hand of Moab!
But Jehoshaphat said, Is there not here a prophet of the LORD, that we may enquire of the LORD by Him? And one of the King of Israel's servants answered and said, Here is Elisha the son of Shaphat, which poured water on Elijah's hands.
And Jehoshaphat said, The word of the LORD is with him. So the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat and the king of Edom went down to him.
And Elisha said unto the king of Israel, What have I to do with thee? get thee unto the prophets of thy father, and to the prophets of thy mother. And the King of Israel said unto him, Nay: for the LORD hath called these three kings together, to deliver them into the hand of Moab.
And Elisha said, As the LORD of hosts liveth, before whom I stand, surely if it wasn't that I regarded the presence of Jehoshaphat the King of Judah, I would not look toward thee, nor see thee.
But now bring me a minstrel. And it came to pass, when the minstrel begin to play, that the hand of the LORD came upon him.
And he said, Thus saith the LORD, make these valleys full of ditches.
And thus saith the LORD, Ye shall not see wind, neither shall ye see rain; yet the valley shall be filled with water, and you shall drink, both ye, and your cattle, and your beasts.
And this is but a light thing in the sight of the LORD : he will deliver the Moabites also into your hand.
And ye shall smite every fenced city, and every choice city, and … fell every good tree, … stop up all the wells of water, … mar every good piece of land with a stone.
And it came to pass in the morning, when the meat offering was offered, … behold, there came water by the way of Edom, and the country was filled with water.
text_no:
La oss se på 2. Kongebok, tredje kapittel, med start i det tiende verset. Vi skal lese noen avsnitt fra Skriften før vi går over til bønnekøen så snart som mulig. For å tydeliggjøre, bønnekøen vil finne sted her, men helbredelsen skjer på Golgata. Vi skal altså be for de syke.
Dette var en viktig tid i Israels historie, en tid for viktige beslutninger, omtrent slik vi står i dag – en splittet og misfornøyd gruppe mennesker. La oss høre:
10 Da sa Israels konge: "Akk, Herren har kalt disse kongene sammen for å overgi dem i Moabs hånd!" 
11 Men Josjafat sa: "Er det ikke en Herrens profet her, så vi kan spørre Herren gjennom ham?" En av kongen i Israels tjenere svarte: "Her er Elisja, Sjaffats sønn, som helte vann over Elias' hender." 
12 Josjafat sa: "Herrens Ord er hos ham." Så gikk Israels konge og Josjafat og Edoms konge ned til ham.
13 Men Elisja sa til Israels konge: "Hva har jeg med deg å gjøre? Gå til din fars og din mors profeter." Men Israels konge sa til ham: "Nei, for Herren har kalt disse tre kongene sammen for å overgi dem i Moabs hånd."
14 Elisja sa: "Så sant Herren, hærskarenes Gud, lever, for hvem jeg står, hadde jeg ikke hatt respekt for Josjafat, Juda konge, ville jeg ikke ha sett på deg eller brydd meg om deg.
15 Men nå, bring meg en harpespiller." Mens harpespilleren spilte, kom Herrens hånd over ham.
16 Og han sa: "Så sier Herren: Gjør denne dalen full av grøfter.
17 For så sier Herren: Dere skal ikke se vind, og dere skal ikke se regn; likevel skal dalen fylles med vann, og både dere, buskapen og dyrene skal drikke.
18 Dette er en enkel sak i Herrens øyne; Han vil også overgi Moabittene i deres hånd.
19 Dere skal slå hver befestede by og hver utvalgte by, felle hvert tre, stoppe alle vannkilder og ødelegge hver god åker med steiner."
20 På morgenen, når matofferløsningen ble ofret, kom vannet fra Edom og fylte landet med vann.
Dette var en betydningsfull tid i Israels historie, ikke ulikt vår tid i dag. La oss fortsette å søke Herren for ledelse og helbredelse.

text:
Let's bow our heads just a moment.
Now, Heavenly Father, faith cometh by hearing, hearing of Thy Word. And now, as we read of this great event in Thy Word, the people have gathered here tonight, Lord, believing that the same God that was with the prophets of old, that was with the Israelites in the journey---wilderness, Daniel in the lion's den, the children in the fiery furnace; the Son of God with the apostles down through the age is still the same God tonight.
Now, we pray for a blessing from Him, that as we poor, unworthy, but needy children stand in His presence, humbly confessing our sins that we have done wrong, and ask forgiveness. And pray, God, that if there's anything in our lives, or our way, any unbelief that would keep this meeting tonight from being a success, God, forgive us now. We pray that God's Spirit might have the right-of-way in the meeting tonight, would bless the people, heal them, save the sinners, and get glory out of the service in our effort. For we ask it in Jesus Christ's name, Thy beloved Son. Amen.
text_no:
La oss bøye våre hoder et øyeblikk.
Nå, Himmelske Far, troen kommer av å høre, høre Ditt Ord. Og nå, når vi leser om denne store hendelsen i Ditt Ord, har menneskene samlet seg her i kveld, Herre, og tror at den samme Gud som var med de gamle profetene, som var med israelittene på reisen gjennom ørkenen, med Daniel i løvehulen, barna i den brennende ovnen — Guds Sønn med apostlene gjennom tidene, fortsatt er den samme Gud i kveld.
Nå ber vi om en velsignelse fra Ham, at vi, fattige, uverdige, men trengende barn, som står i Hans nærvær, ydmykt bekjenner våre synder og ber om tilgivelse for alt galt vi har gjort. Vi ber, Gud, at hvis det er noe i våre liv, eller på vår vei, enhver vantro som ville hindre dette møtet fra å bli en suksess i kveld, at Du tilgir oss nå. Vi ber at Guds Ånd må ha fri adgang i møtet i kveld, velsigne folket, helbrede dem, frelse synderne, og få ære ut av vår innsats. For vi ber om dette i Jesu Kristi navn, Din elskede Sønn. Amen.

text:
[Break in tape] … the King of Jerusalem at the time of the Jews. And King Jehoshaphat was the King of Judah, which they had split up.
And when any, like the churches, as long as we have these split-ups in the church, we're never united.
The Indians lost this United States to the white people, because they were unorganized themselves---split up. They was having tribal fights. If they'd have put themselves together, they could've held their ground.
If there's any people I feel sorry for, is the Indian. He's the real American. We are not. We're immigrants. He's the American that God made America.
Recently had a meeting for them up at Carlsbad. I beg your pardon, I forget the name of the tribe. It was the Apaches of San Carlos, Arizona. I'll never forget that night. First time I'd ever prayed for any Indians. Two of them come through, and the Lord revealed what was wrong with them. And I said, "Lord, if You'll heal them, I'll go to the reservation." And He did. And the missionary helped me good to it.
So, I went up to the reservation. There was about, maybe twenty thousand, or more, gathered out that evening. They had a little platform, built like this, right in front of a little church---floodlights. And it was the most beautiful sight you ever seen to see all those little fires built around, the Indians sitting on their little blankets, and their little papooses around (that's the little babies), and all of them. The old fellows there with big, long pipes, smoking, and they were talking.
text_no:
... kongen av Jerusalem på jødenes tid. Og kong Josjafat var kongen av Juda etter at de hadde delt riket.
Når menigheter har slike splittelser, forblir vi uforente. Indianerne tapte USA til hvite innvandrere fordi de selv var uorganiserte og splittet i stammefeider. Hadde de samlet seg, kunne de ha forsvart seg bedre.
Av alle folkeslag føler jeg mest med indianerne. De er de ekte amerikanerne, ikke vi. Vi er innvandrere. De er de amerikanerne som Gud skapte Amerika for.
Nylig hadde jeg et møte for dem i Carlsbad. Jeg beklager, men jeg husker ikke navnet på stammen. Det var Apache-stammen fra San Carlos, Arizona. Jeg glemmer aldri den kvelden, første gang jeg ba for indianere. To kom frem, og Herren åpenbarte hva som feilte dem. Jeg sa: "Herre, hvis Du helbreder dem, skal jeg dra til reservatet." Og det gjorde Han. Misjonæren hjalp meg godt med det.
Så jeg dro til reservatet. Omtrent tjue tusen, kanskje flere, hadde samlet seg den kvelden. De hadde laget en liten plattform foran en liten menighet, belyst med flomlys. Det var et vakkert syn med alle de små bålene rundt, indianerne som satt på teppene sine med små barna rundt seg. De eldre røykte store, lange piper og samtalte.

text:
And when I went to speak… Now, an Indian's a very strange man. Now, he'll stand and listen at you, but he won't make his decision till he's sure.
Billy Paul had some experience with one, here sometime ago. I said, "Billy, giving out those prayer cards, give it to really, the sick people; not just a fellow with a headache, or toothache. And people there dying with cancer. Give it to somebody who's really sick."
He said, "All right."
He went down---and this was at Phoenix. So he was giving out the prayer cards. And an Indian walked along. He said … patted him on the back, said, "Me sick."
He said, "Well, what's the matter with you, Chief?"
He said, "Me sick."
Well, he just turned around and walked away from him.
The chief kept following him with his hands behind him. Directly… The chief was watching them prayer cards getting lower and lower. So he knocked him on the back again.
He turned around and said, "What's the matter, Chief?"
He said, "Me sick."
He said, "Chief, I'm supposed to give these to people who are really sick." Said, "What's your trouble?"
He said, "Me sick."
So, he went on a little farther, and he caught him again. He had about two, or three prayer cards. He said, "Chief, what's your trouble?"
He said, "Me sick."
Billy said, "Then write on the prayer card, 'Me sick.'"---you know like that, "Me sick."
He was sick. He didn't want to tell what was the matter with him, but the only thing he knew… Maybe that was all he could say---somebody told him to say that, you know, "Me sick." That's all he knowed.
I had one got converted one time. He didn't how to shout. So he only knowed two words, and that was "July," and "August." And he run as hard as he could, hollered, "July August! July August! July August!" That's all he knowed how to do. That's the only words he knowed how to say, but he was shouting just as much as we are when we're… That's right. It was to the glory of God. That's all he knowed how to say.
text_no:
Når jeg skulle tale... En indianer er en veldig spesiell person. Han vil stå og lytte, men tar ikke sin beslutning før han er helt sikker.
Billy Paul hadde engang en opplevelse med en, for en tid tilbake. Jeg sa: "Billy, når du deler ut bønnekortene, gi dem til virkelig syke mennesker; ikke bare en person med hodepine eller tannpine. Det er folk der som dør av kreft. Gi dem til noen som virkelig er syke."
Han sa: "Greit."
Dette var i Phoenix. Han delte ut bønnekortene, og en indianer kom forbi. Han klappet Billy på ryggen og sa: "Jeg syk."
Billy sa: "Hva er galt med deg, Høvding?"
Indianeren svarte igjen: "Jeg syk."
Billy gikk bare videre, og indianeren fulgte etter ham med hendene bak ryggen. Etter hvert så høvdingen at det ble færre og færre bønnekort. Han klappet Billy på ryggen igjen.
Billy snudde seg og spurte: "Hva er galt, Høvding?"
Indianeren gjentok: "Jeg syk."
Billy sa: "Høvding, jeg skal gi disse til folk som virkelig er syke. Hva er problemet ditt?"
Igjen svarte høvdingen bare: "Jeg syk."
Billy gikk videre, men høvdingen fulgte etter. Etter å ha delt ut nesten alle kortene, spurte Billy igjen: "Høvding, hva er problemet ditt?"
Indianeren svarte fortsatt: "Jeg syk."
Billy sa da: "Skriv på bønnekortet: 'Jeg syk.'"
Han var syk. Han ville ikke si hva som var galt, kanskje fordi det var det eneste han kunne si. Kanskje noen hadde bedt ham si det: "Jeg syk."
Jeg opplevde en gang at en indianer ble omvendt. Han visste ikke hvordan han skulle rope av glede. Det eneste han kunne si var "Juli" og "August." Så han løp så fort han kunne og ropte, "Juli August! Juli August! Juli August!" Det var alt han kunne si, men han jublet like mye som vi gjør. Det var til Guds ære. Det var de eneste ordene han kjente.

text:
So, in the San Carlos meeting that night, I was speaking. I said, "You people, I'm sorry, I think it's a stain on our flag to send money over to Britain, and around like that. Them people don't get it---the poor." I'm neither Democrat, nor Republican. I'm a Christian, see, but let me tell you, that's a bunch of nonsense. I was right there to see. I was right there with … King sent for me, and I come over. I was in his palace, and so forth. And went down there, and I know them people are half-starved to death. They don't get that money. Russia will be blowing it back to you pretty soon, so just … like Japan did. So, brother, I'm to keep America in America. That's where she belongs.
Let me tell you, friend, when we went over there, and noticed them poor Indians laying there. Some of them, hundreds of them, die every year, because they get just a few nickels a month … or, a few dollars a month, pension. And they can't live on it. And then they come around. They was raising some sheep and they took all them away from them. And, I don't know what they did. Just, the people freeze, starve to death, and everything. And I think that's a disgrace. I do. I'm for the Indian.
text_no:
På det møtet i San Carlos den kvelden holdt jeg en tale. Jeg sa: "Dere folk, jeg beklager, men jeg synes det er en skamplett på flagget vårt å sende penger til Storbritannia og lignende. De fattige der borte får det ikke." Jeg er verken demokrat eller republikaner; jeg er en kristen. Men la meg fortelle dere, det er bare tull. Jeg så det med egne øyne. Kongen sendte bud etter meg, og jeg dro over. Jeg var i hans palass og så videre. Jeg dro ned dit, og jeg vet at de menneskene sulter halvt i hjel. De får ikke de pengene. Russland vil snart blåse det tilbake til dere, akkurat som Japan gjorde. Så, bror, jeg mener at Amerika bør ta vare på sine egne. Det er der vi hører hjemme.
La meg fortelle deg, venn, når vi dro dit og så de fattige indianerne ligge der. Noen av dem, hundrevis hvert år, dør fordi de bare får noen få mynter, eller noen få dollar i måneden i pensjon. Og de kan ikke leve av det. Og så kommer de rundt. De oppdretter noen sauer, og de ble tatt fra dem. Jeg vet ikke hva de gjorde. Menneskene fryser, sulter i hjel, og alt mulig. Og jeg synes det er en skam. Jeg støtter indianerne.

text:
And so I said, "Now, I can't help that. I'm just one of the people." I said, "Our fathers pushed you back, and so forth, and took what you had away from you. And take your hunting grounds. And the white man's a murderer to begin with. He run out and shoot the buffalo, not just for meat, but just to see how many he can kill.
And that's the right way. I was a game warden here in Indiana for about seven, or eight years. I know it's the truth. The white man is… When he's unconverted, he's just nothing but a murderer. He just take … kill everything he can get his hands on. Just to shoot to see if he can shoot.
That went pretty deep, but brother, this is the Gospel I'm telling you about up here. It's the truth. It's right. If the law is get five squirrels, he'll get six or seven, eight or ten, if he can get them. That's right. And I know that's his nature, but not an Indian. If he catches one, and there's a bigger one there, he got two of them; he'll turn one loose, and keep the other one. He's a real conservationist.
text_no:
«Jeg sa: 'Nå, jeg kan ikke gjøre noe med det. Jeg er bare en av folket.' Jeg sa: 'Våre fedre fortrengte dere og tok det dere hadde. De tok jaktområdene deres, og den hvite mannen har vært en morder fra begynnelsen av. Han løp ut og skjøt bøffelen, ikke bare for kjøttet, men bare for å se hvor mange han kunne drepe.
Og det er slik det er. Jeg var viltvokter her i Indiana i omtrent sju eller åtte år. Jeg vet at det er sant. Den hvite mannen ... Når han ikke er omvendt, er han ingenting annet enn en morder. Han tar ... dreper alt han kan få hendene på. Bare for å skyte for å se om han kan treffe.
Det stakk dypt, men Bror, dette er Evangeliet jeg forteller deg om her. Det er sannheten. Det er riktig. Hvis loven tillater fem ekorn, tar han seks eller sju, åtte eller ti, hvis han kan. Det er riktig. Jeg vet det er hans natur, men ikke en indianer. Hvis han fanger en, og det er en større der, vil han slippe løs den ene og beholde den andre. Han er en ekte naturverner.»

text:
Now … but anyhow, I said, "They pushed you back. And that's what they've done to you: Pushed you back, and pushed you back. You got a raw deal, but," I said, "I can't help that. I don't set the laws of this nation, but I'm trying to tell you about Somebody, tonight, who'll give you the right kind of a deal---and that's Jesus Christ the Son of God." I said, "He died to save you, just the same as He died to save the rest of them." And I said, "Of course, you have a right to doubt that, right now, I suppose, but if God does prove that I'm telling you the truth, well then, there's no way at all for you to doubt it any longer."
So, I called for a prayer line. And usually, if you call for a prayer line without cards, you know what you get---a stampede. So then, I didn't have no prayer cards. So, I just got in there just a few minutes beforehand. And I said, "We haven't got any prayer cards, so, how many wants to be prayed for? Raise up your hand."
There was just only one interpreter there. Nobody raised up their hand.
I said, "All that wants to be prayed for, line up right down this way."
Nobody lined up.
Well then, the missionary went back in the little church, and she brought out some women she had in there. And here they all come out.
text_no:
Men likevel sa jeg: "De presset deg tilbake. Og det er det de har gjort med deg: Presset deg tilbake, og presset deg tilbake. Du har blitt dårlig behandlet, men," sa jeg, "det kan jeg ikke gjøre noe med. Jeg lager ikke lovene i dette landet, men jeg prøver å fortelle deg om Noen i kveld som vil gi deg en rettferdig behandling—og det er Jesus Kristus, Guds Sønn." Jeg sa, "Han døde for å frelse deg, akkurat som Han døde for å frelse resten av dem." Og jeg sa, "Selvfølgelig har du rett til å tvile på det nå, antar jeg, men hvis Gud beviser at jeg forteller deg sannheten, så er det ingen måte for deg å tvile lenger."
Så kalte jeg på bønnekøen. Vanligvis, hvis du kaller på en bønnekø uten kort, vet du hva du får—en strøm av mennesker. Jeg hadde ingen bønnekort. Jeg kom bare inn noen få minutter før. Jeg sa: "Vi har ingen bønnekort, så hvor mange ønsker å bli bedt for? Rekk opp hånden."
Det var bare én tolk der. Ingen rakk opp hånden.
Jeg sa: "Alle som ønsker å bli bedt for, still opp her."
Ingen stilte opp.
Da gikk misjonæren tilbake i den lille menigheten og hun hentet ut noen kvinner hun hadde der inne. Og her kom de alle ut.

text:
And you women, you ought to see them little Indian babies---the cutest little things. But, what I can't understand, they had them all hanging on pegs, and every one of them looked just exactly alike. How they could tell them apart, I couldn't tell. One would go get one, swing on her back. Little fellow never cried, or nothing, he just sat there. I was playing with one of them.
The lady come through, and as soon as I was trying to talk to her just a few moments, I seen she had a venereal disease. Not because she was immoral---the way she had to live. So I said, "It's a venereal disease."
And the interpreter said… She turned and looked at me, and them black eyes snapped. She looked back at the lady. And the interpreter said, "Is that right?"
Yes, but how did I know it? See. That's what she wanted to know.
I said, "Now, she isn't immoral, she's a true woman. But what it is, it's the way she has to live." But it was a venereal.
So, she wanted the interpreter to ask me if God would heal her.
I said, "Yes, if you believe it." So, I had prayer for her.
Next one coming through was a … oh, glaucomas is of the eyes, which is very familiar amongst the Indians.
text_no:
Og dere kvinner, dere skulle sett de små indianske babyene—de søteste småtassene. Men det jeg ikke kan forstå, er at de hadde dem hengende på kroker, og alle så helt like ut. Hvordan de kunne skille dem fra hverandre, kunne jeg ikke forstå. Én ville gå og hente én, svinge henne på ryggen. Den lille gråt aldri, ingenting, han bare satt der. Jeg lekte med en av dem.
En dame kom forbi, og mens jeg prøvde å snakke med henne et øyeblikk, så jeg at hun hadde en kjønnssykdom. Ikke fordi hun var umoralsk, men på grunn av måten hun måtte leve på. Så jeg sa: "Det er en kjønnssykdom."
Tolkeren sa … Hun snudde seg og så på meg, og de svarte øynene glitret. Hun så tilbake på damen. Tolkeren sa: "Er det riktig?"
Ja, men hvordan visste jeg det? Det var det hun ville vite.
Jeg sa: "Nå, hun er ikke umoralsk, hun er en sann kvinne. Men det er på grunn av måten hun må leve på." Men det var en kjønnssykdom.
Hun ville at tolken skulle spørre meg om Gud ville helbrede henne.
Jeg sa: "Ja, hvis du tror det." Så ba jeg for henne.
Neste som kom var en … å, grønn stær i øynene, som er veldig vanlig blant indianerne.

text:
And the next come through was a little girl---and she was deaf and dumb. I said, "Now, the little girl is deaf and dumb. She had fever sometime ago, and it made her go deaf and dumb.
And the mother of the baby … "That's right. That's exactly right."
And I said, "Do you believe that God will heal the baby?"
"Yes." She believed it.
So I asked the Lord if He'd heal the baby. When I got through, I went, "{snap}."
She turned and looked at me---that little girl.
I said, "Can you hear me?"
And the interpreter begin to speak to her. And she could hear, and she could speak. She started mumbling off something like that.
I said, "Oh, she'll talk better after awhile."
She said, "Hmm. Her talk heap good now."
text_no:
Neste person som kom frem, var en liten jente som var døvstum. Jeg sa: "Denne lille jenta er døvstum. Hun hadde feber for en tid tilbake, og det gjorde henne døvstum."
Mor til barnet bekreftet: "Det stemmer. Helt riktig."
Jeg spurte: "Tror du at Gud kan helbrede barnet?"
"Ja," svarte hun. Hun trodde virkelig det.
Så ba jeg Herren om å helbrede barnet. Da jeg var ferdig, knipset jeg med fingrene {knips}.
Den lille jenta snudde seg og så på meg.
Jeg spurte: "Kan du høre meg?"
Tolkeren begynte å snakke til henne. Hun kunne både høre og snakke, selv om hun mumlet litt.
Jeg sa: "Hun vil snakke bedre etter hvert."
Hun svarte: "Hmm. Hun snakker ganske bra nå."

text:
So, the next one in the line was a little cross-eyed boy. Anyone could see he was cross-eyed. I never seen God turn a cross-eyed child down yet---never.
So, I think what did that, I had such a horror of looking at it. When my little Sharon---my little baby---was dying in the hospital many, many years ago, the little thing suffered so hard, when I got to her, her little blue eyes had crossed. And that just kills me to see a cross-eyed child. I think of that. You know, sometimes you have to grind a flower to get the perfume out of it, don't you.
There, that little fellow looked up at me, his little old coarse, horse-mane hair hanging down. He looked at me---little red face. And I picked up the little fellow. I thought, Now, if God will do that before these Indians, that'll give favor.
I put the little fellow up against me, and I said, "Dear God, please have mercy. I pray Thee that Thou will heal the little fellow. And give favor before these people, that I might tell them about Thy beloved Son."
Of course, the interpreter wasn't interpreting that. They was just standing there, watching and I knew God healed that baby.
I said, "Now…" Before I took him off my shoulder, I said, "Now, have them all look this way." And they looked that way. I said, "Now, if God's healed this little cross-eyed boy, will you believe?"
They was looking at one another, you know. They didn't say a word.
I turned his little face around like that, and his little eyes was normal as anybody here.
My, you talk about a prayer line. I had one, really, in a few moments. Looked like a stampede, with the dust a-flying. They had to throw out…
How many knows Reverend Jack Moore? I guess some of you do; and Gordon Lindsay, and them was up there.
And so, the prayer line was coming.
text_no:
Neste i køen var en liten skjeling gutt. Alle kunne se at han skjelte. Jeg har aldri sett Gud avvise et skjelende barn—aldri.
Da min lille Sharon—min lille baby—døde på sykehuset for mange år siden, led hun så mye at øynene hennes ble skjeling. Det dreper meg å se et skjelende barn. Jeg tenker ofte på det. Du vet, noen ganger må du male en blomst for å få ut parfymen.
Der stod den lille gutten og så på meg, hans grove, hestehår-lignende hår hang ned. Han så på meg med sitt lille røde ansikt. Jeg plukket opp den lille gutten og tenkte, hvis Gud gjør dette foran disse indianerne, vil det gi oss favør.
Jeg holdt den lille gutten mot meg og sa: "Kjære Gud, vær så snill og ha barmhjertighet. Jeg ber Deg helbrede denne lille gutten og gi favør foran dette folket, så jeg kan fortelle dem om Din elskede Sønn."
Selvfølgelig tolket ikke tolken dette. De bare stod der og så på, og jeg visste at Gud hadde helbredet den lille gutten.
Jeg sa: "Nå..." før jeg tok ham ned fra skulderen min, "nå, få dem til å se hit." De så hit. Jeg sa: "Nå, hvis Gud har helbredet denne lille skjelende gutten, vil dere tro?" De så på hverandre, uten å si et ord.
Jeg snudde den lille guttens ansikt rundt, og øynene hans var like normale som noen her.
Å, for en bønnekø vi fikk! På få øyeblikk så det ut som en stampede, med støv som føyk rundt. De måtte kaste noen ut...
Hvor mange kjenner Reverend Jack Moore? Jeg antar at noen av dere gjør det; og Gordon Lindsay, de var der også.
Og så kom bønnekøen.

text:
The next old, Indian woman, they had an awful time getting her quiet. She had two broomsticks, or some kind of sticks, and they had rags wrapped around the top for crutches. She looked to be about seventy-five, or eighty years old. She had long plaits hanging down, and leather plaited in her hair. And she was to be the next one in the line, but they were crowding and pushing so at that platform, they got a bunch of Indians, and stood them up there to keep them down.
And so, there was a young Indian boy jumped over the platform, and jumped up here. And he was going to be the next. Well, Brother Moore couldn't tell him to get back, so Brother Moore is a little, kind of short, stout, Irish fellow. He just picked him up by the ribs, like that, and set him back, to let this woman come on. She was next.
And so, here she come. And the poor, old thing. She'd set these crutches out, and she was … arthritis, you know, just so bad. Bless her old heart. I hope to meet her in glory some day. When she got right up close to me, like that, she looked up at me, and them great big ditches in her face---an old mother. And the tears making their way down through them old ditches. Many a hard day on that desert. An old mother, probably had rocked her babies a many times, and lived on whatever they could get a hold of.
She looked up at me like that, and her lips was quivering. She kind of smiled, batted her eyes. Reached over and got a hold of one crutch, the other one put it in her hand, handed it up to me. Straightened up, went walking off that platform, just like that.
What was it? She wasn't prayed for. Didn't ask to be. God rewarded her for her faith.
Just one more thing on that Indian meeting before I close on
text_no:
Den gamle indianske kvinnen hadde store problemer med å roe seg ned. Hun brukte to pinner med filler surret rundt toppen som krykker. Hun så ut til å være rundt syttifem eller åtti år gammel, med lange fletter og lærflettinger i håret. Hun skulle være den neste i køen, men folk trengte og dyttet så mye rundt plattformen at de måtte få noen indianske menn til å holde orden.
En ung indiansk gutt hoppet over plattformen og stilte seg opp for å være den neste, men Bror Moore kunne ikke be ham gå tilbake. Bror Moore, en kort og kraftig irsk mann, løftet gutten opp, satte ham tilbake, og lot kvinnen komme frem. Hun var nestemann.
Kvinnen kom sakte frem med sine gamle krykker, sterkt plaget av leddgikt. Stakkars gamle kvinne. Jeg håper å møte henne i herligheten en dag. Da hun kom nærmere, så hun opp på meg med dype rynker i ansiktet, og tårene rant nedover disse sprekker. Hun var en gammel mor som sikkert hadde gynget sine barn utallige ganger og overlevd på det lille de kunne finne i ørkenen.
Hun så på meg mens leppene dirret, og så smilte hun og pisket bort noen tårer. Hun grep tak i en av krykkene og rakte den til meg. Deretter rettet hun seg opp og gikk bort fra plattformen, helt uten hjelp. Hva var det? Hun ble aldri bedt for, og hun ba heller ikke om det. Gud belønnet henne for hennes tro.
Dette var nok det siste jeg skulle dele om det indianske møtet før jeg avslutter.

text:
it. It was a long about three o'clock in the morning. I noticed the Indians coming through. They were wet, way up this way. I said, "What's the matter?"
The interpreter said, "They thought, first, it was false, but," said, "now they're going out into the desert, and packing their loved ones. And the ford's way down here about twenty miles. And they're not waiting to get to the ford. They're coming right on through the river with them---coming across like that."
So then, I looked, coming along the line, there was a great big fellow standing there. Robust-looking Indian, his lips real blue. That desert gets cold of a morning like that. And he was just shivering.
I looked. He had a big, wide board with a stick across this way, and a stick across that way. And an old Indian man laying on there with his hands across this stick, and his legs across the other one. And the other man was packing it. No stretchers, just was using that for a stretcher.
And I said, "Do you speak English?"
He said, "Leetle."
And I said, "Aren't you afraid you'll take pneumonia?"
"No. Jesus Christ has take care of me. I bring my father."
Said, "Oh, that's it." I said, "Do you believe if I ask Jesus to heal your father, He'll heal him?"
"Yup. That's why I brought him."
I said, "Pass him on by."
As he went by, I laid my hands over on the old man. I said, "Dear God, an old, ancient father, here. Laying here, old, crippled up, shaking with palsy," like that. I said, "Have mercy on him, God, and heal him, I pray, in Jesus' name."
I said, "Now, take him on. As you believe, so will it be."
I brought another one---the next one coming. Just so tired, I could hardly stand there. I heard somebody, screaming, going on. Looked, and the old man had the board on his own shoulder, going along, waving at everybody, like that. Just walking along.
Just that simple. But, you try to figure it all out---how it happens---and that's the reason you miss it. That's right. They just simply believe.
text_no:
Klokken var omkring tre om morgenen. Jeg så indianerne komme gjennom. De var våte og helt gjennombløte. Jeg sa: "Hva er i veien?"
Tolken svarte: "Først trodde de at det var falskt, men nå drar de ut i ørkenen og bærer med seg sine kjære. Vadestedet er omtrent tjue mil unna, men de venter ikke på å nå vadestedet. De kommer rett gjennom elven, på denne måten."
Jeg så en stor kar stå i køen. Han var en robust indianer med blå lepper. Ørkenen blir kald om morgenen. Han sto der og skalv. Han hadde en stor, bred planke med en pinne på tvers, og en gammel indianermann lå på denne planken med hendene og bena over pinnen. Mannen brukte planken som en båre.
Jeg spurte: "Snakker du engelsk?"
"Leetle," svarte han.
Jeg spurte: "Er du ikke redd for å få lungebetennelse?"
"Nei. Jesus Kristus tar vare på meg. Jeg har med meg faren min."
"Å, slik er det," sa jeg. "Tror du at hvis jeg ber Jesus om å helbrede faren din, vil Han gjøre det?"
"Ja, det er derfor jeg tok ham med."
Jeg sa: "Send ham videre."
Da de gikk forbi, la jeg hendene mine på den gamle mannen. "Kjære Gud, en gammel, eldgammel far, ligger her, gammel og krøplet, skjelvende med lammelse," ba jeg. "Ha nåde for ham, Gud, og helbred ham, ber jeg, i Jesu navn."
"Ta ham med videre. Som du tror, så skal det være."
De neste kom. Jeg var så trøtt at jeg nesten ikke kunne stå. Plutselig hørte jeg noen skrike og lage styr. Jeg så at den gamle mannen nå bar planken på sin egen skulder, vinkende til alle. Han gikk bortover, bare slik.
Det er så enkelt. Men når du prøver å finne ut hvordan det skjer, mister du det. Det er riktig. De tror bare helt enkelt.

text:
Now, that's the simplicity of faith. But Israel had got away from God in these times, and faith was very scarce.
So, there was a man, Jehoshaphat, who was a righteous man---the King of Judah. And he went up. And King Ahab, you know how bad he was. And Elijah, the prophet, had prophesied concerning him, and about the garden that he had taken to. Jezebel had killed Naboth, taken his vineyard. And Elijah said, "The dogs will lick your blood." A prophet sent from God told him that the dogs would lick his own blood. Because how he had done this righteous man, and cheated, and took his vineyard.
And then, here his son come along---Jehoram---taken the place of his father, and was a very wicked man. Still worshipping the idols of his mother, Jezebel, a little Egyptian princess. Marrying out of their ranks, that's what did it. It'll do it in your home, too. Marry amongst unbelievers and see what takes place. You'll lose your victory right there.
text_no:
Det er enkelheten av tro. Men Israel hadde vendt seg bort fra Gud i disse tider, og tro var svært sjelden.
Det var en mann, Josafat, en rettferdig mann og kongen av Juda. Han dro opp til kong Akab, kjent for sin ondskap. Profeten Elia hadde profetert om Akab og hagen han hadde tatt. Jezebel hadde drept Nabot og tatt vingården hans, og Elia sa: "Hundene skal slikke blodet ditt." En profet sendt fra Gud fortalte ham at hundene skulle slikke hans eget blod på grunn av det han hadde gjort mot denne rettferdige mannen ved å bedra og ta vingården hans.
Akabs sønn, Joram, tok deretter farens plass og var en svært ond mann. Han fortsatte å tilbe sin mors, Jezebel, avguder. Jezebel, en liten egyptisk prinsesse, hadde giftet seg utenfor deres rekker, og det var dette som førte til deres fall. Det samme kan skje i ditt eget hjem. Gifter du deg med en ikke-troende, vil du miste seieren.

text:
Watch.
Then, when they went out, and Jehoshaphat… What a slip-up on him, to make an alliance with such a person as that fellow---an unbeliever. He got in the same trouble we did when we made an alliance with Russia, and sent them over all of our planes, and things, during the other war. Now, they're going fly them back, and shoot them at us, see. "How can two walk unless they be agreed?"
We wouldn't accept the cross of Christ that was preached, and now we got a double-cross. That's exactly right.
Same thing taken place there. And there Jehoshaphat went out with the King Jehoram, the King of Jerusalem. And they went out to make war with the kings of the Philistines, or, Moabites, rather. And they went seven days… Now look, all tied up in business. Didn't stop to consult God.
When you come to the meeting tonight… If you're sick, did you come, say, "Now Father, I'm going down there. I ask you to be merciful to me, and let my faith be raised up, tonight, to a place where I can believe Your Son, and be healed." Did you think to pray? When you heard the meeting was going to be up here at Connersville, did you pray? Go around, get the neighbors, and have little prayer meetings? We forget those things, and that's why we get in trouble.
text_no:
Se.
Da de dro ut, Jehoshaphat... For en tabbe av ham å inngå en allianse med en slik person---en vantro. Han havnet i samme problemer som vi gjorde da vi inngikk en allianse med Russland og sendte dem alle våre fly og utstyr under den andre krigen. Nå kommer de til å fly dem tilbake og skyte på oss. "Hvordan kan to vandre sammen uten å være enige?"
Vi ville ikke akseptere Kristi kors som ble forkynt, og nå har vi fått en dobbeltfare. Det er helt riktig.
Det samme skjedde der. Og der dro Jehoshaphat ut med kong Jehoram, kongen av Jerusalem. De dro ut for å kjempe mot kongene av filisterne, eller moabittene, snarere. Og de vandret i syv dager... Se, helt opptatt av forretninger. De stoppet ikke for å rådspørre Gud.
Når du kommer til møtet i kveld... Hvis du er syk, kom du da og sa: "Far, jeg skal ned dit og ber Deg være barmhjertig mot meg og la min tro bli opphøyet i kveld, slik at jeg kan tro på Din Sønn og bli helbredet." Husket du å be? Da du hørte at møtet skulle være her i Connersville, ba du da? Tok du runden til naboene og hadde små bønnesamlinger? Vi glemmer de tingene, og det er derfor vi får problemer.

text:
They went seven days out into the wilderness; never fetched no compass. And come to find out, they got in trouble. They went without consulting God, and all the water supply was cut off.
That's what's the matter with the church tonight; the water supply is cut off. Trying to carry the church on without the supernatural. Trying to carry the church on just in its natural. Trying to pattern after the other churches of the world and that's the reason we're not having the blessings we used to have.
Someone said, "I'd walk fifty miles on cobblestones, barefooted, to get to a good old-fashioned, Holy-Ghost revival again."
What's the matter? God's still God! You haven't consulted Him lately. Pray! That's the only way to find…
We used to sing a little song: "Pray, pray, the only way to reach higher grounds. Pray, pray, the prayer of faith will bring God's blessings down." That's the only way to do it.
text_no:
De gikk sju dager ut i ødemarken uten å bruke kompass. Til slutt oppdaget de at de hadde kommet i trøbbel. De dro uten å rådføre seg med Gud, og all vanntilførsel ble kuttet av.
Det er det som er galt med menigheten i kveld; vanntilførselen er kuttet av. Vi forsøker å drive menigheten uten det overnaturlige og baserer oss bare på det naturlige. Vi etterligner andre menigheter i verden, og det er grunnen til at vi ikke opplever de velsignelsene vi pleide å ha.
Noen sa: "Jeg ville gått femti mil på brostein, barføtt, for å komme til en god, gammeldags, Hellig-Ånd-revival igjen."
Hva er galt? Gud er fortsatt Gud! Du har ikke rådført deg med Ham nylig. Be! Det er den eneste måten å finne...
Vi pleide å synge en liten sang: "Be, be, den eneste måten å nå høyere grunn på. Be, be, troens bønn vil bringe Guds velsignelser ned." Det er den eneste måten å gjøre det på.

text:
All right.
But they went without consulting God, and found out their water supply was cut off. They were in the middle of the desert, dying. So there had to be enough religion in one of them to think about consulting God, and that was Jehoshaphat, the King of Judah, a righteous man. He said, "Isn't there a prophet of the Lord somewhere that we could consult about this thing?"
Kind of late to think of it then, wasn't it? "Seek ye first the kingdom of God, and His righteousness, and all other things will be added."
Said, "Isn't there one somewhere?"
And one of his servants came and said, "Yes. Here is Elisha, who poured water on Elijah's hands." My! Must rub off a little bit, now and then.
text_no:
Men de dro av gårde uten å rådføre seg med Gud og oppdaget at vannforsyningen var kuttet. De var i ørkenen og holdt på å dø. Blant dem måtte det være en med nok religiøs innsikt til å tenke på å rådføre seg med Gud, og det var Josjafat, kongen av Juda, en rettferdig mann. Han sa: "Er det ikke en profet fra Herren her som vi kan spørre om dette?"
Det var litt sent å tenke på det da, var det ikke? "Søk først Guds rike og Hans rettferdighet, så skal alt dette bli gitt dere i tillegg."
Han spurte: "Er det ikke én her et sted?"
En av tjenerne svarte: "Ja, her er Elisa, som helte vann på Elijas hender." Ja, det smitter vel litt av, fra tid til annen.

text:
I remember one night, I got called down on that. I said, "All you Christians sit where the sinners are. It's just like smallpox, it'll break out all over you."
About two night after that, in the same tent revival, back when I was just still in the Baptist revivals; there was a little woman got up, with her hair combed back so slick, her face was shining like a peeled onion. She said, "I've heard preachers say … sit next to you, so it would rub off." She said, "Bless God, it never rubbed off on me." Said, "I paid the price, like the rest of them did, and got it."
I thought, Oh, my! Let me keep still.
That's right. It don't rub off. You have to be willing to receive it.
text_no:
Jeg husker en kveld jeg ble irettesatt for det. Jeg sa: "Alle dere kristne, sitt der hvor synderne sitter. Det er som kopper, det sprer seg overalt."
To kvelder senere, under det samme teltmøtet, da jeg fortsatt deltok i de baptistiske stevner, reiste en liten kvinne seg. Med glattkjemmet hår og et ansikt som skinte som en skrellet løk, sa hun: "Jeg har hørt forkynnere si … sitt ved siden av deg, så vil det smitte." Hun sa: "Velsignet være Gud, det smittet aldri på meg." Hun fortsatte: "Jeg betalte prisen, akkurat som de andre gjorde, og fikk det."
Jeg tenkte: Åh, kjære! La meg holde meg stille.
Det stemmer. Det smitter ikke. Du må være villig til å ta imot det.

text:
But Elisha had poured water on Elijah's hands. What a marvelous prophet. He had a double portion.
I like that Elijah and Elisha in type. Let's type it a minute. Look. Elijah, the old prophet, represented Christ. Elisha represents the church---the young prophet.
Now, when they started, I want you to notice their journey. When Elijah found Elisha, he was working in the field. He took his mantel, and threw over him, and said, "Follow me."
And he killed the ox, made a feast for the poor, and followed Elijah.
And when Elijah said, "I've got to go up to Dothan." So when he started up, he told the young prophet, said, "You stay back."
He said, "As the Lord lives, and your soul never dies, I'll not leave you." I like that. Stay right with it.
And he went on up. And he said, "Now, you stay here. I've got to go up to the school of the prophets." Second stage of the journey.
text_no:
Men Elisja hadde øst vann på Elias' hender. For en vidunderlig profet. Han fikk en dobbelt del av ånden.
Jeg liker symbolikken mellom Elias og Elisja. La oss se på det et øyeblikk. Elias, den gamle profeten, representerte Kristus. Elisja representerer Menigheten - den unge profeten.
Legg merke til reisens start. Da Elias fant Elisja, arbeidet han på marken. Elias tok sin kappe, kastet den over Elisja og sa: "Følg meg." Elisja drepte oksene, laget et måltid for de fattige og fulgte Elias.
Elias sa: "Jeg må dra opp til Dotan." Da han begynte reisen, sa han til Elisja: "Bli her." Elisja svarte: "Så sant Herren lever, og din sjel aldri dør, vil jeg ikke forlate deg." Jeg liker det. Hold deg trofast.
Så fortsatte de videre. Elias sa: "Nå, bli her. Jeg må dra opp til profetskolen." Dette var den andre etappen av reisen.

text:
First stage---Martin Luther stage---first stage of the journey. Second stage was the school of the prophets. Elijah went up there and he said to Elisha, "Now you stay here, I pray thee, for the Lord is calling me to Jordan."
But Elisha was really a prophet. He said, "As the Lord lives, and your soul never dies, I'll not leave you." I like that. Second stage of the journey on. God always has did that.
Notice. Then he said, "I'm going to Jordan."
And Elisha… Some of the prophets there at the school said, "You know your master's head will be taken from you today?"
He said, "I know it, but hold your peace." He knew what was going to take place, so he followed him.
And he got down to Jordan---perfect type of the church. The first stage of the journey was justification through Martin Luther---Dothan. The second stage of the journey was John Wesley at the school of the prophets---sanctification. Now, he's going to Jordan---dying-out time. Jordan means "death, passing over." We're entering out of all church ages.
text_no:
Første fase – Martin Luther-fasen – den første fasen av reisen. Andre fase var profetskolen. Elia gikk dit og sa til Elisja: "Nå blir du her, jeg ber deg, for Herren kaller meg til Jordan."
Men Elisja var virkelig en profet. Han sa: "Så sant Herren lever, og din sjel aldri dør, vil jeg ikke forlate deg." Det liker jeg. Andre fase av reisen er i gang. Gud har alltid gjort det slik.
Legg merke til. Deretter sa han: "Jeg går til Jordan."
Og Elisja… Noen av profetene der ved skolen sa: "Vet du at din mester blir tatt bort fra deg i dag?"
Han svarte: "Jeg vet det, men vær stille." Han visste hva som skulle skje, så han fulgte med ham.
Da de kom til Jordan – en perfekt parallell til menigheten. Den første fasen av reisen var rettferdiggjørelse gjennom Martin Luther – Dothan. Den andre fasen av reisen var John Wesley ved profetskolen – helliggjørelse. Nå skal han til Jordan – det er tiden for å dø fra seg selv. Jordan betyr "død, overgang." Vi er på vei ut av alle kirkens tidsaldre.

text:
I know I have Methodists, Baptists, and everything, sitting here. I have not one thing against you. God knows my heart. You're my brothers, same as any of the rest of them. And I am a member of the Baptist church myself. But I just found something good I'm trying to tell you about, something that goes just a little deeper.
You say, "Is there deeper?"
Certainly. And still more deeper coming. Don't get crystallized.
In the old journey of the children of Israel, every time, they watched that pillar of fire. Is that right? And when that pillar of fire stopped, they stopped and built under it. They were under the fire. And then, there was a thousand priests watched that. And every time that pillar of fire would move---if it was ten o'clock in the day, ten o'clock at night, noontime, whenever it was---they blasted these trumpets, and Israel broke camp and followed the light. Is that right? All right.
text_no:
Jeg vet at jeg har metodister, baptister og andre blant oss her. Jeg har ingenting imot dere. Gud vet mitt hjerte. Dere er mine brødre, akkurat som alle andre. Jeg er selv medlem av baptistmenigheten. Men jeg har bare funnet noe godt jeg prøver å fortelle dere om, noe som går litt dypere.
Du spør, "Finnes det noe dypere?"
Absolutt. Og det kommer stadig mer. Ikke bli fastlåst.
I den gamle reisen til Israels barn fulgte de alltid ildsøylen. Er det riktig? Når ildsøylen stanset, stoppet de og bygde leir under den. De var under ilden. Og da var det tusen prester som voktet. Hver gang ildsøylen beveget seg—om det var klokken ti på dagen, ti på natten, midt på dagen, når som helst—blåste de basunene, og Israel brøt leir og fulgte lyset. Er det riktig? Bra.

text:
Everywhere in the stage of the journey the pillar of fire went, Israel followed. If it went this way, they went that way. If it went that way, they went that way. Right hand, or left, they followed on.
Notice. Beautiful type. In the first reformation, there was a little man by the name of Martin Luther, who got his eyes opened in the Scripture. And he knowed the just shall live by faith. And the pillar of fire began to move out of that fifteen hundred years of Dark Ages. And Martin Luther saw the pillar of fire moving; he blasted the Gospel trumpet and went out with it---built under it. But the thing of it was, Luther organized a church.
This is going to hurt. Well, brother, just like my mother… I think she's here tonight. When I was a little kid, we had to eat … just take our meat skins that we'd get from the store, and put it in a bread pan and render it out, and make the lard and mix up some meal in there, and make hoecakes. I don't know whether you know what they are, or not.
The horrible thing was, is every Saturday night, we had to take a big dose of castor oil. I can't even stand to hardly say the name. I'd hold my nose; I'd gag. I'd say, "Mama, I just can't take that stuff. It makes me so sick."
She said, "Honey, if it don't make you sick, it don't do you any good."
So I say that's the same thing tonight. If this to make you right good and sick, it won't help you, maybe.
All right.
text_no:
Overalt på reisen fulgte Israel ildsøylen. Flyttet den seg til høyre, fulgte de til høyre. Flyttet den seg til venstre, fulgte de til venstre. Uansett hvilke vei den gikk, fulgte de etter.
Merk. Et vakkert forbilde. I den første reformasjonen var det en mann ved navn Martin Luther, som fikk øynene åpnet for Skriften. Han visste at "den rettferdige skal leve ved tro". Ildsøylen begynte å bevege seg ut av de femten hundre årene med mørketid, og Martin Luther så dette. Han blåste i evangeliets trompet og fulgte etter, bygget under det. Men problemet var, Luther organiserte en kirke.
Dette kommer til å gjøre vondt. Vel, Bror, som min mor… Jeg tror hun er her i kveld. Da jeg var liten, måtte vi ofte ta kjøttrester fra butikken og smelte dem for å lage smult. Vi blandet smultet med mel for å lage hoecakes. Jeg vet ikke om du vet hva det er.
Det verste var at hver lørdag kveld måtte vi ta en stor dose lakserolje. Jeg kan knapt stå ut å si navnet. Jeg holdt for nesen, brakk meg og sa: "Mamma, jeg kan bare ikke ta dette. Det gjør meg så kvalm."
Hun sa: "Kjære, hvis det ikke gjør deg kvalm, vil det ikke gjøre deg godt."
Så jeg sier det samme i kveld. Hvis dette gjør deg riktig kvalm, hjelper det deg kanskje ikke.
Greit.

text:
God never did deal with organizations. God deals with men. The Holy Ghost never fell on organizations; It fell on men. That's right.
Notice. The first organized church is the mother church---the Catholic church. The rest of them are offsprings from her.
Now, but the word church means "called out, separated."
Now, notice. Then Luther---the pattern---he organized the Lutheran church. And one day, the pillar of fire moved out. And Luther was so organized, and had his rituals and things, he couldn't move with it.
text_no:
Gud har aldri forholdt seg til organisasjoner. Gud forholder seg til mennesker. Den Hellige Ånd falt aldri på organisasjoner; Den falt på mennesker. Det stemmer.
Legg merke til dette. Den første organiserte menigheten er modermenigheten—den katolske kirke. De andre er avkom fra henne.
Ordet menighet betyr "kalt ut, atskilt."
Legg merke til dette. Luther—eksempelet—organiserte den lutherske kirken. En dag beveget ildsøylen seg videre, og Luther var så organisert med sine ritualer og skikker at han ikke kunne bevege seg med den.

text:
So there was a little fellow in England named John Wesley. He saw the pillar of fire moving, and away he went with it, and preached sanctification. Luther couldn't preach sanctification, because he preached justification. So Wesley began to preach sanctification. And away he went. The fire moved on.
Well, then, the first thing you know, Wesley become so organized. You either had to be Wesleyan Methodist, or you was nothing---you was not in it. That's right. And then, off come these other little springs from it.
And one day, the pillar of fire began to move away, and the Pentecostal group saw it. So, Wesley couldn't take speaking in tongues, and gifts of the Spirit, and things. He had to stay there, he'd taught against it. So, he was organized so tight he couldn't move. And the pillar of fire moved on away from him, and the Pentecost moved with it. That's right.
But now, here. Now the Pentecost is organized so tight, the pillar of fire's moving again, and they can't move. That's just exactly right. Amen! But, brother, God's fire is moving just the same! [Break in tape.] … just the same.
text_no:
Det var en liten kar i England som het John Wesley. Han så ildsøylen bevege seg og fulgte den, og begynte å forkynne helliggjørelse. Luther kunne ikke forkynne helliggjørelse fordi han forkynte rettferdiggjørelse. Så Wesley begynte å forkynne helliggjørelse, og ildsøylen beveget seg videre.
Så skjedde det som ofte skjer: Wesley ble så organisert at man enten måtte være Wesleyansk metodist, eller man var ikke med. Det stemmer. Og så oppsto noen andre små avleggere fra dette.
En dag begynte ildsøylen å bevege seg igjen, og pinsebevegelsen la merke til det. Wesley kunne ikke akseptere tungetale og Åndens gaver; han måtte bli der han var, siden han hadde lært imot det. Han var så stramt organisert at han ikke kunne bevege seg. Og ildsøylen beveget seg bort fra ham, og pinsebevegelsen fulgte med. Det stemmer.
Men nå er pinsebevegelsen også blitt så stramt organisert at ildsøylen beveger seg igjen, og de kan ikke bevege seg. Akkurat. Amen! Men, bror, Guds ild beveger seg uansett [Tomt område på lydbånd]... akkurat det samme.

text:
Look here. The organized church was Moses, the lawgiver. And the lawgiver Moses glorified himself instead of God, and wasn't permitted to take the children over Jordan.
And the church today is glorifying themselves. "I belong to the Assemblies." "I belong to Church Of God." "I belong to this, that, or the other."
What are you anyhow? That's right. It's the truth!
Now, there's good men in every one of them. We are brethren. Break down that thing
But, look. A man that had been with them all along, Joshua---a type of the signs and wonders. He was the one who was permitted. Not the church group, not the organization, but Joshua was pulled out, and took the children of Israel across Jordan. That's right. Amen.
text_no:
Se her. Den organiserte menigheten representerte Moses, lovgiveren. Og Moses, lovgiveren, herliggjorde seg selv i stedet for Gud og fikk ikke tillatelse til å føre barna over Jordan.
Dagens menighet herliggjør seg selv. "Jeg tilhører Assemblies." "Jeg tilhører Church Of God." "Jeg tilhører dette, det eller det andre."
Hva er du egentlig? Det er sant! Det er sannheten!
Nå, det finnes gode menn i hver og en av dem. Vi er brødre. Bryt ned den barrieren.
Men se her. En mann som hadde vært med dem hele tiden, Josva—et tegn på under og mirakler. Han var den som fikk tillatelse. Ikke menighetsgruppen, ikke organisasjonen, men Josva ble utvalgt og førte Israels barn over Jordan. Det er riktig. Amen.

text:
Notice, back to my Scripture… I feel like I could almost preach tonight. Look. Now, look here. When he went the first trip… When he found the church, the first stage down there was justification, John Wesley … I mean, Luther's age. Second stage: The school of prophets---sanctification---Wesley's age, schools and organizations and so forth. Third stage: Pentecost---crossing the river.
Notice, when he got there, there stood the Jordan before him. He took off his mantel, struck the water, and went across. And he said, "Now … (oh, my!) now that you've crossed, keep your eye on me. Now, after you've come through, and separated everything, severed yourself, keep your eye on me."
We got too many crossed-eyed Christians (if you want to take it that way)---one eye on Christ, and the other one on the world. Take it off! Look to Him only!
"I'll see what my neighbor says about it." I'll see what this guy says."
What do I care what they say? They don't save you. God saves you. Keep your eye on Him.
text_no:
Legg merke til, tilbake til min Skrift... Jeg føler at jeg nesten kunne forkynne i kveld. Se her. Når han dro på den første reisen… Når han fant menigheten, den første fasen der nede var rettferdiggjørelse, John Wesley... Jeg mener, Luthers tid. Andre fase: Profetskolen—helliggjørelse—Wesleys tid, skoler og organisasjoner og så videre. Tredje fase: Pinse—over elven.
Legg merke til, når han kom dit, sto Jordan foran ham. Han tok av sin kappe, slo på vannet og gikk over. Og han sa, "Nå… (å, min!) nå som du har krysset, hold øye med meg. Nå, etter at du har kommet gjennom og skilt alt, skilt deg ut, hold øye med meg."
Vi har for mange mennesker som ikke klarer å velge (hvis du vil si det slik)—ett øye på Kristus, og det andre på verden. Ta det bort! Se bare til Ham!
"Jeg vil se hva naboen min sier om det." Jeg vil se hva denne personen sier."
Hva bryr jeg meg om hva de sier? De frelser deg ikke. Gud frelser deg. Hold øye med Ham.

text:
Across the river they went. Said, "What will you ask?"
I wish we had time to take up before this---the law of adoption. Pentecost was born (that's right) like the son, but that same son was born in a family had to be adopted also. That's right.
There's where you missed your calling, friends. When the adoption time come, if the tutor that raised the child found the child not worthy, they absolutely still remained a son, but missed their adoption. But, if they was adopted into the family, then they was full fellowship with… Any check that they wrote was just as good as what their daddy wrote.
That's what's the matter today, brother. The church ought to be in adoption right now, bringing into the full favors of God. But the thing, we're ups-and-downs, and ins-and-outs, and ups-and-downs, and ins-and-outs till God can't trust us with nothing, hardly.
"One day I belong to the Assemblies, the next day I belong to something else. And I'm Methodist the next day, and a Baptist next day."
You pack your paper from church to church. And every little group that you go into the group, "I'm in the Baptist now. And the first thing you know, somebody makes me angry, the deacon gets angry with me, the pastor says something I don't believe. I pick up my paper, and take off over to the Methodist. If the Methodist don't get them, I take them down, go down to the Assemblies, or I'd go to the Church of God." You've packed your paper till it's wore out. Get your name written in the Lamb's book of life, and forget about it. That's right.
I've been in the Branham family forty-four years, and they never did ask me to join the family. I was born in it! I'm a Branham by birth! And you're a Christian by birth! Hallelujah! You don't join the family of God, you're born in the family of God. That's right.
All right.
text_no:
De gikk over elven. De spurte: "Hva vil du be om?"
Jeg skulle ønske vi hadde tid til å diskutere dette før—adopsjonsloven. Pinsebevegelsen ble født (det stemmer) som en sønn, men den samme sønnen måtte også adopteres inn i en familie. Det er korrekt.
Der har dere misset deres kall, venner. Når adopsjonstiden kom, hvis læreren som oppdro barnet fant barnet uverdig, forble de fortsatt en sønn, men de gikk glipp av adopsjonen. Men hvis de ble adoptert inn i familien, hadde de full rett til alle privilegier. En hvilken som helst sjekk de skrev, var like god som en faren deres skrev.
Det er problemet i dag, brødre. Menigheten burde være i adopsjon nå, og bringe seg selv inn i Guds fulle gunst. Men vi er opp-og-ned, inn-og-ut, opp-og-ned, inn-og-ut, slik at Gud knapt kan stole på oss med noe som helst.
"En dag tilhører jeg Forsamlingene, neste dag tilhører jeg noe annet. Jeg er metodist den ene dagen og baptist den andre."
Du bærer papirene dine fra menighet til menighet. Og i hver gruppe sier du: "Jeg er i baptistmenigheten nå. Og før du vet ordet av det, blir jeg sint på en diakon eller pastoren sier noe jeg ikke er enig i. Jeg tar med meg papirene og drar til metodistene. Hvis metodistene ikke passer, tar jeg dem til Forsamlingen, eller jeg går til Guds Menighet." Papirene dine er så utslitt. Få navnet ditt skrevet i Lammets bok og glem papirene. Det er riktig.
Jeg har vært en del av Branham-familien i førtifire år, og de har aldri bedt meg om å bli med i familien. Jeg ble født inn i den! Jeg er en Branham ved fødsel! Og du er en kristen ved fødsel! Halleluja! Du blir ikke medlem av Guds familie, du blir født inn i Guds familie. Det stemmer.
Amen.

text:
Notice. He said, "Ask me what you will, now, and I'll give it to you."
Watch. He wasn't afraid to ask. He said, "I want a double portion of your spirit." Amen. I like that. Ask abundantly. Ask for great things. Believe for great things. Said, "A double portion of your spirit."
Said, "You've asked a hard thing, but nevertheless, if you see me when I go, you'll have it."
Oh, my. I tell you, he had one single thought. That prophet didn't get out of his view. He stayed right with him, watching him. On he went. On he went, up the mountain, down through the valley; up the mountain, down through the valley, but he kept his eye on that prophet.
After a while, they was up at the top of the hill, and down from heaven come a chariot of fire, scattered them apart. And Elijah jumped up on the chariot, looked back to Elisha, pulled off his mantel and threw it back to him, and away he went into heaven.
Elisha took that mantel, and walked down to the Jordan, stood there. And there was the school of prophets over there, looking at him to see what he'd do. He took that mantel, and doubled it back and forth, and struck the Jordan, said, "Where is the God of Elijah?" And she divided one way, and the other way.
A beautiful type of the church, who's come down through the Lutheran age, the Methodist age, and through Pentecost age, and now crossing the Jordan.
text_no:
Legg merke til. Han sa: "Be Meg om hva du vil, nå, og Jeg skal gi det til deg."
Se. Han var ikke redd for å be. Han sa: "Jeg vil ha en dobbel del av din ånd." Amen. Jeg liker det. Be overflødig. Be om store ting. Tro på store ting. Han sa: "En dobbel del av din ånd."
Han sa: "Du har bedt om noe vanskelig, men hvis du ser meg når jeg går, skal du få det."
Å, min. Jeg forteller dere, han hadde én eneste tanke. Den profeten gikk ikke ut av hans syn. Han holdt seg rett ved ham og fulgte ham. Oppover fjellet og ned gjennom dalen; oppover fjellet og ned gjennom dalen, men han holdt øye med profeten.
Etter en stund var de oppe på toppen av høyden, og ned fra himmelen kom en ildvogn og skilte dem. Og Elias hoppet opp på vognen, så tilbake på Elisja, tok av seg sin kappe og kastet den tilbake til ham, og så for han opp til himmelen.
Elisja tok kappen, gikk ned til Jordan og sto der. Og der sto profetens disipler og så på hva han ville gjøre. Han tok kappen, foldet den frem og tilbake, slo Jordan og sa: "Hvor er Elias’ Gud?" Og elven delte seg til begge sider.
Et vakkert bilde på menigheten, som har kommet ned gjennom lutherske, metodistiske og pinsebevegelser og nå krysser Jordan.

text:
Keep your eyes on Jesus.
Watch! Jesus was a type of the church. He was standing one day, and a woman run up, said, "I want my son to sit on one side, and one on the other."
Said, "Can you drink the cup that I drink?"
She said, "Yes, Lord."
Said, "Truly you can, but can you be baptized with the baptism that I'm baptized with?"
Said, "Yea, Lord."
Said, "Truly, you can. But the right hand and left hand is not Mine to give, but My Father which is in heaven."
Now, He gathered them up there, and blessed them just before they went away. And He said, "Tarry ye in the city of Jerusalem until you're endued with power from on high." "The things that I do shall you also, and greater than this shall you do." A double portion was promised to the church. And I tell you, they went to the upper room, and stayed there till the day that Jesus was taken up. And the same baptism of the Holy Ghost that He had on Him fell on the Pentecostal people! Hallelujah! There's your double portion!
text_no:
Hold øynene festet på Jesus. Legg merke til! Jesus var en type for menigheten. En dag stod Han der, og en kvinne kom løpende og sa, "Jeg vil at min sønn skal sitte på den ene siden av Deg, og den andre på den andre siden."
Jesus sa, "Kan du drikke det begeret som Jeg drikker?"
Hun svarte, "Ja, Herre."
Han sa, "Sannelig, du kan, men kan du bli døpt med den dåpen som Jeg er døpt med?"
Hun svarte, "Ja, Herre."
Jesus fortsatte, "Sannelig, du kan. Men høyre og venstre hånd er ikke Min å gi, men Min Faders som er i himmelen."
Deretter samlet Han dem og velsignet dem like før de dro. Han sa, "Bli i byen Jerusalem til dere blir fylt med kraft fra det høye." "De ting Jeg gjør, skal dere også gjøre, og større enn dette skal dere gjøre." En dobbel del ble lovet menigheten. De gikk til øvre rom og ble der til den dagen Jesus ble tatt opp. Og den samme dåpen i Den Hellige Ånd som Han hadde på Seg, falt på pinsefolket! Halleluja! Der har du din dobbelte del!

text:
Why, you people who claim to kiss the rim of the cup of the golden blessings of God, who could sit still in such a day (My!) when you've got a double portion among you? Where is the God that was on Jesus Christ? Where is He at? The One who knowed the thoughts of the people's mind, the one who did this, that, or the other."
